textByHash.16c1609ac3e3419a4ffac0f7f5a595f8d5c7a400: I see. If you are an enemy of the Devourer and her corrupt scabarites, then you are a friend to me. I am Mehhar, and I can help you.
textByHash.612e5318863e011777b1f150609174cea8ea18e7: Even when I still lived, the desert hid many secrets. Now, centuries later, it hides even more. Of all those secrets, few are greater than the ones hidden in that tomb.
textByHash.9ea0e9e766b0ae6de4ca0a61ac0cee79abc1eba4: It is the resting place of my father, Osmumten. He was once Pharaoh of these lands. My destiny was to succeed him, but the usurpers saw an end to that.
textByHash.758ab3bf4316da0cb096790e5e4e6d84d110e38a: Being a Pharaoh offers many luxuries, both in life and in death. Many strive only for these luxuries. They forget that being a Pharaoh also comes with responsibility.
textByHash.6aaaa43c99ee7a4cb8641e3d572ce67303783e07: My father had a responsibility greater than most. At the time of his rule, our people were locked in a great war. To hold back the Ruinous Powers, our gods granted us immensely powerful weapons.
textByHash.72cc42a2110c1e3ec6129c7f211c50f8ca189802: When the war was over, my father was tasked with hiding those weapons, lest they fall into nefarious hands. Two of them were buried with him. They will be what the Devourer seeks.
textByHash.813989b4e556c46e3d899343046036ff7327081c: I trust you are aware of what the Devourer desires?
textByHash.e4b50503f6f741c44bc02676b02b897f90c051d1: Yes. When the war ended and the Devourer rose from the ashes, she did so with only one goal. She will not stop until every soul, living or dead, has been destroyed.
textByHash.fb24a3c8914a7112ea62117650041c0da04eee0f: Many would call the Devourer a god, but they speak only half truths. She is a demi-goddess of limited power, and does not possess the means to devour souls on a mass scale.
textByHash.ed0c37a1c152b06a02403ab28dc2e45d3630e0c6: The weapons in my father's tomb can be used to drain a being of divine energy and transfer that energy to another. If used correctly, they would grant the Devourer true godhood.
textByHash.2a8a529ac1a34a53c0917d0cbccfd6ce3be86662: Before you leave, I ask that you remember your original purpose here.
textByHash.5ff0d15d97f0ea836b80685fb850c3b576d7bbb4: Yes. They have made their home here in my tomb, disturbing my rest. From the chamber above, the High Priest of Scabaras commands them.
textByHash.81f1e737093c1e85bde0b7d26342f34811e1432a: He believes he does so in the name of Scabaras. He does not realise that he is actually a puppet of the Devourer.
textByHash.17e1eb67e9bc346a44dbcd9dc623f6562a9f9fe7: Free him of her control, and the Devourer will be without a powerful resource. It will also allow me to rest once more.
textByHash.7817503f6b0bbd095531e8ae424836a2d6241b1e: When the Devourer first emerged, Icthlarin himself taught us an incantation to free an individual from her control. I would suggest you use it, rather than risking harm to the High Priest.
textByHash.a7f7f19140005856ecd88b1a6729890480cbbb55: 'Majaan, Manand, Almuk.' Be warned, while it will work on those she has hypnotised, it will have no effect on her corrupted akhs. They are sadly beyond saving.
textByHash.d82b16f6e4046ab4983206f56d19c255151146b5: You will find a key to the upper chamber in an urn to the back of this room. Now go, with the blessing of Scabaras.
textByHash.4f0d86eef033aed476e5cae3c5d435d9f400d6e2: In life I was Mehhar, son of Osmumten. To me, my death was mere moments ago. To you on the other hand, I am surely considered ancient.
textByHash.33f7692fef3ae26019ca2993e6ba5e2fd0565616: I sense that the gods have long since departed this world, but when I lived, they still walked among us.
textByHash.0c2048dd346ed59430ac41add2fe84bf9871f73c: My father ruled during the great war. While the war was eventually won, many considered the cost too great. My father led us with the support of the gods, yet he was the one people blamed.
textByHash.e89d7a4963b19a4586691720c076faf87ca09d0a: Him, and the rest of my family. I would have died as well, were it not for the bravery of my father's servants. They gave their lives, so that I might live.
textByHash.2476257b20004896fa7c91b1c929d38f6ee22861: To Ullek? Yes. I found solace here with a group dedicated to Scabaras. In time, I became their leader, and the Sect of Scabaras was formed.
textByHash.27daa5d2f4d899287faf1e8ba0bf1ca80a635a12: That is no surprise. Such is the fate of all things. Still, while the sect would have eventually decayed, it was the Devourer's corruption that quickened its demise.
textByHash.92bc3c315278892ce20dd2a1167d9642859ee914: Don't be. You have a chance to free the High Priest of Scabaras. If you do, the sect may yet be reborn.
textByHash.931cae073e69e6691d0b1fd5798bbfe9783a790b: The one you call Amascut died long ago. In her place, the Devourer was born.
textByHash.8fc44eccce6e9dd8d342db2663104bf2cc1021cf: Only Icthlarin himself knows the true answer to that question.
textByHash.255a92933bbee5ad48404672797f70ceb9e94765: During the great war, our forces were decimated by the servants of the Ruinous Powers. To turn the tide, Icthlarin and Amascut sought powerful allies to aid us.
textByHash.c126b029360eddb8adddb2a2c4f899660a790005: They found them far from here, in a place beyond this world. A powerful race of faceless ones. They became Icthlarin's Stern Judges.
textByHash.008e23cf9249b4d8d2370b62fd4ab476fd23c78a: Upon her return, Amascut vanished. She played no further part in the war. Only when the fighting was over did she reappear, but it wasn't really her at all.
textByHash.7eae2e50e252165d56518fbd1b4cdceb1deea4fa: It started during the rule of my grandmother. An ever-expanding empire to the north set their sights on our lands. War soon broke out.
textByHash.3dff6830428116c5a6d74b04408b53299813bb68: The armies of the Ruinous Powers were far stronger than our own. That was until Icthlarin's Stern Judges joined the fray. Thanks to them, the war was seemingly won.
textByHash.06d3da9c3e4c7ab65060ca1edc16b115b4b713c0: No. Among the Stern Judges, there was a serpent. After my father became Pharaoh, this serpent convinced the others to abandon us.
textByHash.b7686d6fb7f055aa3240e4bb1d3b9b0e00dff70e: They did. We were powerless against a force of that might. Nothing could stop their advance.
textByHash.7cf550421cae7192135c0fdf15da05539a0eb74f: It was Tumeken himself. He sacrificed himself to annihilate the enemy. The cost was great, but the war was won.
textByHash.fe648104585e6af0f0a27cd7e3403a51cf8a8bf2: That's a matter of perspective. I died long ago, and yet we are still having this conversation.
textByHash.7107d8fcd18bcb6f12e04c241e5d2944e880c3fa: While Tumeken himself may be gone, he lives on through his four avatars. While they walk among us, Tumeken will always be at our side.
textByHash.383cd0ad15298bea944f108c2e019ad3e39b822c: You will find a key in an urn at the back of this room. May Scabaras guide you to success.
textByHash.5cf63a0438ff5b3f47b31de24f7304dbf52b142a: Have you freed the High Priest of Scabaras yet?
textByHash.cba020e4eae330666c66714aa932fc6939c71441: I sense that the Devourer's corruption has been lessened. I trust you were successful?
textByHash.c6d061de7ff5837fa175b57d2ca35fa42aae048e: That was my hope, but while the High Priest is free, many scabarites have been lost to the Devourer. I sense that they will soon descend on my tomb, hoping to reclaim it.
textByHash.055d8e55b521e1be79228907fe2806a349311caf: Continue your fight against the Devourer. Only with her defeat, will we finally find freedom.
textByHash.141b744abf0f67e463554dca4baa6a9207d04bf1: That was my hope, but while the High Priest is free, many scabarites have been lost to the Devourer. As you will have seen, they have descended on my tomb, hoping to reclaim it.
